title: Podman doesn't compress image layers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**

Pushing with podman doesn't compress the image layers. I use Github Container registry.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman push --compress <tag> <dest>` (I tried also different compression formats)

2. `docker push <tag>`


Dockerfile

```
FROM docker.io/node:16-alpine
WORKDIR /app
```

**Describe the results you received:**

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@StarpTech Please attach the full debug log; you have chosen precisely the irrelevant part (copying of the image config, which is never compressed).

---

A few general notes:

- `"mediaType": "application/octet-stream",` will not be touched; the semantics of that object is unknown, so Podman does not know whether it is OK to compress it, and what `mediaType` to use to represent the outcome of the compression. How did `application/octet-stream` ended up there in the first place? That’s not valid for container images.
- As a general principle, pushes prefer to use existing on-registry blobs over spending the time and bandwidth to upload a compressed artifact. That behavior can’t currently be disabled.

Please try it yourself. It is very easy to reproduce with my steps above. I'd expect that podman behaves like docker. This behavior is totally unexpected because I specified compression which isn't applied.

> As a general principle, pushes prefer to use existing on-registry blobs over spending the time and bandwidth to upload a compressed artifact. That behavior can’t currently be disabled.

When uncompressed, this has an impact on all images that use the image as a base. It would be great to control this with a flag.

`podman push` is not going to use `application/octet-stream` in ordinary usage. `docker.io/node:16-alpine` does not use that either.

So the steps to reproduce, already fairly minimal, are almost certainly missing a critical element.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mtrmac I can't follow you. As shown in my example. Podman behaves differently than docker.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mtrmac This is waiting for a response from you.
No, this needs _complete_ steps to reproduce or at least a debug log.
